name: Cassia Vorne
background: A Court interrogator who dismantled House Valdris through procedural means — subpoenas, witnessed confessions, and archived testimony. She considers herself a professional. She is entirely correct. The distinction between what she does and what a villain does is, to her, a matter of documentation.
lore: A Court interrogator who dismantles houses through paperwork and patience. She does not need to threaten — the documentation does it for her.

name: Tomas Rell
background: Court enforcer lieutenant running muscle for House Aurevaine's enforcement contracts. Practical, not cruel — cruelty wastes time and leaves witnesses in bad moods. He has been doing this long enough to know exactly how little of himself to spend on any given problem.
recruitCondition: Defeated in a confrontation; offer him a contract with terms he can respect and payment that arrives on time
lore: Muscle for hire on a Court contract. Professional enough not to enjoy the work, experienced enough not to need to.

name: Petra Ashford
background: Court debt collector who works through administrative tools first, physical ones only when the ledger demands it. She has a list. Every name on it is there for a specific, documented reason. She finds disorder genuinely offensive.
recruitCondition: Defeated; offer her a position managing accounts, with clear scope and access to proper records
lore: Debt collector who brings both the ledger and the consequences. She finds disorder genuinely offensive.

name: Gast Morrow
background: Senior Compact enforcement warden. Old guard. Runs the Warrens night patrol like he owns it, because functionally he does. He started in this district and has never left — not ambition, not ideology, just the specific authority that comes from outlasting everyone who tried to move him.
lore: A Compact warden who has outlasted every investigation into his conduct. The Warrens are his district in every sense that matters.

name: Lenna Vath
background: Register compliance specialist for the Civic Compact. Sends people to the holding cells for paperwork reasons. She has never raised her voice. She has never needed to. The gap between a filed complaint and a detention order is, in her hands, approximately four working days.
lore: She has never raised her voice. A filed form is slower than a blade and leaves fewer questions.

name: Cort Heller
background: Junior Compact enforcer who wants to make sergeant. He follows orders he should question, not because he's afraid to think but because thinking has never been rewarded where he works. He is not a bad person. He is a person in a system that will make him one if he stays.
recruitCondition: Defeated; show him a better path than sergeant — something that rewards his competence without requiring him to stop thinking
lore: A junior enforcer still learning to stop thinking. That education is progressing on schedule.

name: Rack
background: Ring enforcer running the Pale protection circuit. No surname given, none asked. Not a killer by preference — he prefers impressions that last, because dead men don't pay next month and they also stop being afraid of you. He has a reputation that precedes him by several blocks.
recruitCondition: Defeated in combat; offer him work with a clear chain of command and no requirement to explain himself to people below him
lore: Tallow Ring protection on the Pale circuit. He prefers impressions that last — corpses stop paying rent.

name: Sira Cole
background: Ring logistics coordinator who knows where every cache is between the Harbor and the Hollows. She didn't build the network — she inherited a mess and organized it into something that works. The Ring relies on her in ways it doesn't fully acknowledge because acknowledging it would mean paying her more.
recruitCondition: Outmaneuvered in a logistics conflict; offer her actual authority over supply operations and a cut that reflects what she contributes
lore: The Ring's logistics coordinator for the Harbor-to-Hollows run. She organized a mess into a network they depend on without admitting it.

name: Dovel
background: Ring fixer who handles disputes the broker network can't resolve. One name. No traceable origin. He doesn't threaten — he arrives, listens until everyone is uncomfortable, and then tells you what is going to happen. He is usually right. His fee is nonnegotiable in both directions.
lore: A Ring fixer who resolves disputes the brokers can't. He arrives, listens, and tells you what will happen. He is usually right.

name: Oric Fell
background: Gilded Hand field commander. Carries no personal grievance against anyone — grievance is unprofessional and it compromises tactical thinking. He was given objectives, he has resources, and he will achieve results. The fact that those results may ruin someone is a contract problem, not a moral one.
lore: Gilded Hand field commander. No personal grievance, only objectives and resources. The damage is a contract outcome, not a choice.

name: Nem
background: Gilded Hand infiltration specialist. Has been inside three organizations the player will interact with. What she found, she has kept — not as leverage, exactly, but as insurance against the day her employer decides she knows too much. She doesn't trust easily and has excellent reasons not to.
recruitCondition: Outmaneuvered or exposed; offer her a contract with genuine operational independence and no requirement to report everything she knows
lore: Gilded Hand infiltrator who has been inside three organizations and kept what she found. She does not trust easily. She has reasons.

name: Katch
background: Gilded Hand heavy. Simple priorities: complete the job, get paid, eat a real meal. He is not stupid — he just doesn't see the point in complexity when directness works. He has been working enforcement contracts since he was seventeen and has never once been surprised by where it led.
recruitCondition: Defeated in combat; offer him a fair wage and work that is straightforward — no politics, no complications
lore: Gilded Hand muscle. Straightforward work, done cleanly. He has been on enforcement contracts since seventeen and is not surprised by where they lead.

name: Mael Forn
background: Director of the Ashen Compact. He worked for House Valdris once, before — he doesn't specify before what. He knows what the house was. He has complicated feelings about it that express as organizational ambition: build something better, or at least something that survives. He finds the player's existence irritating and instructive in roughly equal measure.
lore: Director of the Ashen Compact, and former House Valdris employee. He doesn't specify what changed. He is building something that survives.

name: Sven Weld
background: Ashen Compact field operative. Competent, slightly bored. He has done this kind of work for three different organizations and the work is never different — only the justification changes. He is not disillusioned so much as just very tired of the narrative part.
recruitCondition: Defeated on a field operation; offer him work that is genuinely interesting for once — he will consider it
lore: Ashen Compact field operative. He has worked three organizations and the work is always the same — only the justification differs.

name: Rhen
background: Ashen Compact social operative. Knows which doors open for money and which open for information, and has a working theory on how to convert one currency into the other. One name, by preference. She finds titles useful but personally ridiculous.
recruitCondition: Outmaneuvered socially; demonstrate that the player's network has doors hers doesn't, and offer access
lore: Ashen Compact social operative. She knows which doors open for money and which for information, and how to convert between them.

name: Blaise Ruk
background: Corrupt senior Compact official who takes a cut from both the Ring and the Court. Has survived four internal investigations. The investigations were not incompetent — he is simply very good at presenting the correct documentation and very patient about waiting for the investigators to be reassigned. He does not think of himself as corrupt. He thinks of himself as realistic.
lore: A Compact official who takes a cut from the Ring and the Court both. He has survived four investigations. He does not think of himself as corrupt.

name: Marsh
background: Mercenary turned opportunist operating in the Hollows. Used to be better than this — not morally, necessarily, just more purposeful. He took a contract five years ago that went wrong in ways he didn't see coming, and he has been improvising in the same general direction since. He is tired in a way that sleep doesn't fix.
recruitCondition: Defeated; offer them work and a way out of the Hollows
lore: Mercenary turned opportunist in the Hollows. He took a contract that went wrong and has been improvising since. He is tired in a way that sleep doesn't fix.
